join_order.build: subquery_m: num filtered (uom=pure, value IS NOT NULL) → GROUP BY (adsh,tag) MAX(value)
join_order.probe: outer num filtered rows (same uom=pure, value IS NOT NULL predicates — single scan)
qualifying outer num rows from step 1
join_order.key: adsh_code (== sub_row_index directly, no hash needed)

filters.implementation: load num/uom_dict.txt at runtime; find pure_code by string match; filter num.uom[i] == pure_code
direct: sub_fy[adsh_code] == 2022  (adsh_code is sub row index)
probe max_map[(adsh<<32|tag)] == value using open-addressing hash map

scan_strategy.note: Single scan: collect qualifying rows {adsh_code, tag_code, value} into thread-local vectors. Both subquery m and outer query use same filter — no second scan of num needed.

max_map.probing: bounded linear: for(int p=0; p<cap; p++) { idx=(slot+p)&(cap-1); ... } per C24
max_map.build_phase: sequential after parallel scan merge (39k rows — trivial)

qualifying_rows_buffer.type: thread_local vector<tuple<int32_t adsh, int32_t tag, double value>>
qualifying_rows_buffer.merge: concatenate thread-local vectors after parallel scan; ~39k total rows

parallelism.thread_local_reduction: each thread collects qualifying rows into local vector; merge after scan completes
parallelism.rationale: Only ~39k rows qualify from 39M scan; build/join/sort phases are trivially fast on small result

aggregation.thread_safety: build sequentially after parallel scan merge (tiny 39k rows)

indexes.purpose: adsh→sub_row lookup; since adsh_code==sub_row_index, lookup is O(1) array access
indexes.note: No mmap needed for the index itself — just use adsh_code directly as sub row index

output.sort_strategy: std::partial_sort on ~39k candidates for top-100 (O(n log k), per P6)

actions: load num/uom_dict.txt → find pure_code by string match (C2)
parallel morsel scan (64 threads, morsel=100k rows)
per row: if uom[i]==pure_code && !isnan(value[i]) → push {adsh[i], tag[i], value[i]} to thread-local vector
merge thread-local vectors (sequential, trivial for ~39k rows)
for each row in merged: key=(adsh<<32|tag), update max_map[key]=max(current, value)
for each qualifying row {adsh, tag, value}:
1. probe max_map[key]: skip if value != max_map[key]
3. collect {adsh, tag, value} into result vector
std::partial_sort result vector by (value DESC, name ASC, tag ASC) for top-100

correctness_checks.C2: Load uom_dict.txt at runtime; never hardcode pure_code
correctness_checks.C15: max_map key includes BOTH adsh_code AND tag_code
correctness_checks.C18: Decode tag and name via dict[code], not raw int
correctness_checks.C20: Use std::fill for sentinel init, NOT memset
correctness_checks.C24: Bounded probing: for(int p=0; p<cap; p++) loop, not unbounded while
correctness_checks.P1: Open-addressing hash map for max_map, not std::unordered_map
correctness_checks.P4: Single scan of num (both subquery and outer use same filter)
